title: Checks, Captures, Threats
subtitle: A routine for every move
summary: Train the habit every strong player uses: before you move, look at the checks, captures, and threats.
attribution: Original prose; a widely-taught beginner thinking routine.

steps.title: Before every move
steps.markdown: Strong players run a quick checklist before moving: look at every **[[check]]**, every **[[capture]]**, and every **[[threat]]** — for *both* sides. It only takes a few seconds and it stops most blunders. Let's practise.

steps.title: Checks first
steps.markdown: Always look at [[check|checks]] first — a check can end the game. Black's king is stuck behind its own pawns. Find the checkmate with your rook!

hints.text: Which empty line can your rook reach the back rank on?
Slide the rook all the way up.

feedback.correct: Checkmate! The king is trapped by its own pawns — a classic back-rank mate.
feedback.wrong: Look for a check the king can't escape. Your rook can reach the 8th rank.

steps.title: Then captures
steps.markdown: No checks here, so look at [[capture|captures]]. Black's queen has wandered next to your pawn. Take is a mistake — *unless* it wins material. This time it does!

hints.text: Your e4 pawn can capture diagonally.

feedback.correct: Yes! A pawn just won a queen. Always check whether a capture wins material.
feedback.wrong: Look at what your pawn can capture diagonally.

steps.title: "To take is a mistake"
steps.markdown: There's an old saying: *to take is a mistake*. It doesn't mean never capture — it means **stop and think first**. When you spot a tempting [[capture]], what should you ask?

options.label: Is it really free, or is it a trap?
options.explain: Exactly. Check what recaptures, and whether grabbing it loses time or walks into a fork.

options.label: Nothing — always grab free material
options.explain: Careful! Many 'free' pawns are bait. Always look at your opponent's reply first.

steps.title: Make it a habit
steps.markdown: From now on, before every move, whisper it to yourself: **[[check|checks]], [[capture|captures]], [[threat|threats]]** — for both sides. Do that and you'll spot the wins and dodge the traps.
